hero.desc: I share my frontend learning journey, work experience and real code examples through this blog.

purpose.title: What is this site for?
purpose.desc1: karimov.dev is my personal frontend blog and a project that will gradually evolve into a full portfolio platform. Here I document the technologies I’m learning, share real code examples, and build a structured knowledge base for myself.
purpose.desc2: The main goal of this site is to organize my experience, refine my development style, and eventually showcase real projects in the portfolio section. Every post focuses on practical value: a problem, a solution, and a clear takeaway.
purpose.desc3: This platform also serves as a place where people can learn more about me, explore my work, and get in touch for collaboration opportunities. Over time, I will expand it with more articles, categories, and production-level projects.

footer.desc: Personal platform where I share my frontend journey, practical experience and future portfolio projects.
footer.telegramDesc: Join the channel to receive new articles and updates first.

blog.title: Blog
blog.desc: Useful articles about Frontend, Next.js and TypeScript.
blog.empty: No posts found in this category.

about.name: Karimov Kamron
about.heroText: I am a frontend developer currently studying at PDP Academy and close to graduation. I have been learning programming for more than two years and continue improving every day. I studied C++ at a beginner level which helped me build strong algorithmic thinking. My main focus now is web frontend — building clean, modern and responsive user interfaces. My goal is to work at a strong tech company. Working at companies like Google is one of my long-term ambitions.
